item.title: ERDNet: Efficient Ship Object Detection in Haze Environment
item.dc:creator: Yu Zhang
Pengcheng Guo
Wenqiang Li
Moran Ju
Qing Hu
item.description: Ship object detection faces the challenge of increasing the difficulty of positioning in hazy environments. Additionally, the latest convolutional neural network (CNN) cannot obtain satisfactory detection results. Therefore, we propose ERDNet, a dual-branch-driven end-to-end network, to improve ship detection accuracy during hazy weather. Specifically, we design a two-branch feature extraction network through complementary attentional fusion to enhance the object feature information of low-quality images. Second, we designed a feature pyramid fusion structure called ERPSA-PAN to aggregate context information effectively. ERPSA-PAN improves the feature fusion capability of the model by suppressing background interference and enhancing useful information. In addition, the spatial-frequency fusion block (SFFB) module with expanded receptive fields is added to the ERDNet detection head to improve the detection ability for multiscale targets. More importantly, we design a robust haze loss to handle different degrees of haze. We introduce two new haze ship datasets, Hazy-SeaShips and Hazy-Boats, which include 17,000 synthetic haze images and 2898 real haze images, respectively, to address the lack of hazy ship image datasets. The images cover variations such as haze thicknesses, ship types, and scales, along with complex backgrounds, and occlusions. The experimental results show that the proposed method is superior to other state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods and achieves relatively competitive results. The source codes, and datasets are available on https://github.com/ZikHH/ERDNet. Published: 2026-04-21T19:51:35+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.813 (must_read)

Authors: Yu Zhang; Pengcheng Guo; Wenqiang Li; Moran Ju; Qing Hu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3684409

Relevance: 0.813 (must_read)

Ship object detection faces the challenge of increasing the difficulty of positioning in hazy environments. Additionally, the latest convolutional neural network (CNN) cannot obtain satisfactory detection results. Therefore, we propose ERDNet, a dual-branch-driven end-to-end network, to improve ship detection accuracy during hazy weather. Specifically, we design a two-branch feature extraction network through complementary attentional fusion to enhance the object feature information of low-quality images. Second, we designed a feature pyramid fusion structure called ERPSA-PAN to aggregate context information effectively. ERPSA-PAN improves the feature fusion capability of the model by suppressing background interference and enhancing useful information. In addition, the spatial-frequency fusion block (SFFB) module with expanded receptive fields is added to the ERDNet detection head to improve the detection ability for multiscale targets. More importantly, we design a robust haze loss to handle different degrees of haze. We introduce two new haze ship datasets, Hazy-SeaShips and Hazy-Boats, which include 17,000 synthetic haze images and 2898 real haze images, respectively, to address the lack of hazy ship image datasets. The images cover variations such as haze thicknesses, ship types, and scales, along with complex backgrounds, and occlusions. The experimental results show that the proposed method is superior to other state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods and achieves relatively competitive results. The source codes, and datasets are available on https://github.com/ZikHH/ERDNet.

item.title: Linear attention goes global in molecular dynamics
item.dc:creator: Sheng Gong
Wen Yan
item.description: A new attention mechanism brings long-range awareness to machine learning force fields with linear cost and preservation of symmetry. The method offers a flexible alternative to existing long-range modules, including fragmentation-based interactions and physics-based long-range fixes. Published: 2026-04-21T09:03:21+00:00 Venue: Nature Machine Intelligence Score: 0.801 (must_read)

Authors: Sheng Gong; Wen Yan

Journal: Nature Machine Intelligence

A new attention mechanism brings long-range awareness to machine learning force fields with linear cost and preservation of symmetry. The method offers a flexible alternative to existing long-range modules, including fragmentation-based interactions and physics-based long-range fixes.

item.title: Towards Adaptive Open-Set Object Detection via Category-Level Collaboration Knowledge Mining
item.dc:creator: Yuqi Ji
Junjie Ke
Lihuo He
Lizhi Wang
Xinbo Gao
item.description: Existing object detection methods struggle to generalize across increasingly data domains while simultaneously adapting to the emergence of novel categories. To tackle this challenge, adaptive open-set object detection (AOOD) has been introduced, which employs supervised training on base categories within the source domain while enabling unsupervised adaptation to both base and novel categories in the target domain. However, existing AOOD approaches are still hindered by several limitations, including insufficient cross-domain feature representation, inter-category ambiguity in novel classes, and inherent feature bias toward the source domain. To overcome these issues, this paper proposes a category-level collaboration knowledge mining strategy designed to comprehensively exploit both inter-class and intra-class feature relationships across domains. Specifically, a clustering-based memory bank (CMB) is initially constructed to aggregate class prototype features, class auxiliary features, and intra-class disparity features, thereby embedding rich category-level knowledge into a unified memory structure. The CMB is iteratively updated through unsupervised clustering, which facilitates the modeling of intra-category relationships and enhances its capacity for cross-domain knowledge representation. Subsequently, a base-to-novel selection metric (BNSM) is designed to identify features corresponding to novel categories within the source domain by regulating the relationships between the novel categories and each base category. The selected features are then leveraged to initialize the object detector for the classification of novel categories. Finally, an adaptive feature assignment (AFA) strategy is introduced to transfer the learned category-level knowledge to the target domain, enabling the assignment of category labels to features. The memory bank is updated asynchronously with these assigned features to mitigate source domain bias. Extensive experiments conducted on ... Published: 2026-04-20T20:07:06+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.798 (must_read)

Existing object detection methods struggle to generalize across increasingly data domains while simultaneously adapting to the emergence of novel categories. To tackle this challenge, adaptive open-set object detection (AOOD) has been introduced, which employs supervised training on base categories within the source domain while enabling unsupervised adaptation to both base and novel categories in the target domain. However, existing AOOD approaches are still hindered by several limitations, including insufficient cross-domain feature representation, inter-category ambiguity in novel classes, and inherent feature bias toward the source domain. Specifically, a clustering-based memory bank (CMB) is initially constructed to aggregate class prototype features, class auxiliary features, and intra-class disparity features, thereby embedding rich category-level knowledge into a unified memory structure. The CMB is iteratively updated through unsupervised clustering, which facilitates the modeling of intra-category relationships and enhances its capacity for cross-domain knowledge representation. Subsequently, a base-to-novel selection metric (BNSM) is designed to identify features corresponding to novel categories within the source domain by regulating the relationships between the novel categories and each base category. The selected features are then leveraged to initialize the object detector for the classification of novel categories. Finally, an adaptive feature assignment (AFA) strategy is introduced to transfer the learned category-level knowledge to the target domain, enabling the assignment of category labels to features. The memory bank is updated asynchronously with these assigned features to mitigate source domain bias. Extensive experiments conducted on ...

item.title: Efficient and Accurate Object Detection with Asymmetric Progressive Semi-Decoupled Head and Harmonic Focal Loss
item.dc:creator: Bo Han
Lihuo He
Junjie Ke
Jiehao Tang
Di Wang
Xinbo Gao
item.description: Efficiently and accurately recognizing interesting objects within the image and regressing bounding boxes to enclose them has been a persistent pursuit in object detection. However, existing detectors fail to achieve both aspects simultaneously due to insufficient task interaction and suboptimal classification behavior. To solve the problem, this paper proposes a novel detector with Efficient Asymmetric Progressive Semi-Decoupled Head (EAPSDH) and Harmonic Focal Loss (HFL). Specifically, we generalize the detection head into a progressive asymmetric paradigm that performs hierarchical and dynamically recalibrated interaction between classification and localization, enabling iterative mutual enhancement in an efficient manner beyond the prior designs. Meanwhile HFL is proposed to improve classifier optimization by addressing the imbalance between positive and negative samples. HFL dynamically increases the loss weights of positive samples, amplifying their gradient contributions during classifier training, which significantly reduces classification error. By jointly improving task-specific feature representation and classification optimization, EAPSDH and HFL complement each other to alleviate the inconsistency between classification and localization performance, resulting in an efficient and accurate one-stage detector termed EADet. Experimental results on the MS COCO database demonstrate that EADet effectively mitigates the inconsistency between classification and localization performance. Furthermore, EADet achieves a strong trade-off between accuracy and speed, reaching 47.4 AP at 33.2 FPS on the MS COCO with ResNet-101 under the 2× training schedule, demonstrating its effectiveness compared with recent state-of-the-art detectors. Code will be available at https://github.com/HB-X/EADet. Published: 2026-04-20T20:07:06+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.792 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Bo Han; Lihuo He; Junjie Ke; Jiehao Tang; Di Wang; Xinbo Gao

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3683599

Relevance: 0.792 (must_read)

Efficiently and accurately recognizing interesting objects within the image and regressing bounding boxes to enclose them has been a persistent pursuit in object detection. However, existing detectors fail to achieve both aspects simultaneously due to insufficient task interaction and suboptimal classification behavior. To solve the problem, this paper proposes a novel detector with Efficient Asymmetric Progressive Semi-Decoupled Head (EAPSDH) and Harmonic Focal Loss (HFL). Specifically, we generalize the detection head into a progressive asymmetric paradigm that performs hierarchical and dynamically recalibrated interaction between classification and localization, enabling iterative mutual enhancement in an efficient manner beyond the prior designs. Meanwhile HFL is proposed to improve classifier optimization by addressing the imbalance between positive and negative samples. HFL dynamically increases the loss weights of positive samples, amplifying their gradient contributions during classifier training, which significantly reduces classification error. By jointly improving task-specific feature representation and classification optimization, EAPSDH and HFL complement each other to alleviate the inconsistency between classification and localization performance, resulting in an efficient and accurate one-stage detector termed EADet. Experimental results on the MS COCO database demonstrate that EADet effectively mitigates the inconsistency between classification and localization performance. Furthermore, EADet achieves a strong trade-off between accuracy and speed, reaching 47.4 AP at 33.2 FPS on the MS COCO with ResNet-101 under the 2× training schedule, demonstrating its effectiveness compared with recent state-of-the-art detectors. Code will be available at https://github.com/HB-X/EADet.

item.title: Disentangling representation and cross-domain adaptation for unsupervised change detection in multimodal remote sensing images
item.dc:creator: Erlei Zhang
Xinyu Li
He Zong
Yuan Gao
Jinchang Ren
item.prism:publicationName: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
item.description: Change detection in multimodal remote sensing images presents significant challenges, particularly for abrupt damage assessment. Multimodal images (e.g., SAR vs. optical) have different physical characteristics, making it difficult to detect land cover changes through direct comparison. Existing approaches typically rely on supervised learning (requiring costly labeled data), one-to-one modality mappings (which struggle with complex real-world modality variations), or structure similarities of modality-specific features (limiting generalizability). In this paper, we propose a novel unsupervised Disentangled Representation with Unpaired cross-domain Adaptation (DRUA) framework to address these issues. DRUA disentangles raw images into style representations (modality-specific attributes) and content (land cover features). It uses style filters to enable flexible many-to-one/one-to-many mappings, capturing modality-specific knowledge without paired data. By aligning content domains via maximal projection optimization of the raw image, DRUA facilitates direct comparison for change identification. This approach eliminates reliance on unchanged regions. Extensive experiments on five multimodal remote sensing datasets have demonstrated that DRUA can achieve superior change detection performance against ten state-of-the-art methods. By effectively disentangling both the content and style information of heterogeneous images, DRUA has attained a mean overall accuracy (OA) of 96%, an F1 score of 0.79, and an average Kappa coefficient (KC) of 0.77, all outperforming the second-best method by about 2% in OA, 0.18 in F1 score, and 0.19 in KC. The datasets used in this study and related code resources are available online at: https://github.com/sighdn/DRUA . Published: 2026-04-20T14:26:02+00:00 Venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Score: 0.791 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Erlei Zhang; Xinyu Li; He Zong; Yuan Gao; Jinchang Ren

Journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1016/j.isprsjprs.2026.04.011

Relevance: 0.791 (must_read)

Change detection in multimodal remote sensing images presents significant challenges, particularly for abrupt damage assessment. Multimodal images (e.g., SAR vs. optical) have different physical characteristics, making it difficult to detect land cover changes through direct comparison. Existing approaches typically rely on supervised learning (requiring costly labeled data), one-to-one modality mappings (which struggle with complex real-world modality variations), or structure similarities of modality-specific features (limiting generalizability). In this paper, we propose a novel unsupervised Disentangled Representation with Unpaired cross-domain Adaptation (DRUA) framework to address these issues. DRUA disentangles raw images into style representations (modality-specific attributes) and content (land cover features). It uses style filters to enable flexible many-to-one/one-to-many mappings, capturing modality-specific knowledge without paired data. By aligning content domains via maximal projection optimization of the raw image, DRUA facilitates direct comparison for change identification. This approach eliminates reliance on unchanged regions. Extensive experiments on five multimodal remote sensing datasets have demonstrated that DRUA can achieve superior change detection performance against ten state-of-the-art methods. By effectively disentangling both the content and style information of heterogeneous images, DRUA has attained a mean overall accuracy (OA) of 96%, an F1 score of 0.79, and an average Kappa coefficient (KC) of 0.77, all outperforming the second-best method by about 2% in OA, 0.18 in F1 score, and 0.19 in KC. The datasets used in this study and related code resources are available online at: https://github.com/sighdn/DRUA .

item.title: Box-Supervised 3D Instance Segmentation with Level Set Evolution and Cross-View Consistency
item.dc:creator: He Wang
Guofeng Zhang
item.description: Weakly supervised 3D instance segmentation aims to reduce the high cost of point-wise annotations while maintaining competitive accuracy compared with fully supervised methods. Among various weak annotations, box annotation offers an ideal trade-off between labeling efficiency and supervision strength. However, most box-supervised methods rely on a two-stage training pipeline: 1) generating pseudo-labels, 2) training the segmentation model with pseudo-labels, which is iterative and sensitive to pseudo-label quality. To address this issue, we propose an end-to-end framework that directly learns instance masks from box annotations without an explicit pseudo-label generation and iterative relabeling and retraining stage. Specifically, we introduce a boundary-aware refinement module that adaptively learns instance boundaries from boxes through level set evolution. Furthermore, we propose a multi-scale geometric augmentation module to alleviate semantic ambiguity in overlapping regions by applying cross-view consistency constraints on predictions. Finally, we construct a multi-objective optimization framework, which improves both the training stability of level-set-evolution-based boundary refinement and the overall segmentation performance. Extensive experimental results on both indoor and outdoor datasets demonstrate that our method achieves the SOTA performance across multiple benchmark datasets with different backbones and closely approaches fully supervised counterparts. Published: 2026-04-21T19:51:35+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.788 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: He Wang; Guofeng Zhang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3684400

Relevance: 0.788 (must_read)

Weakly supervised 3D instance segmentation aims to reduce the high cost of point-wise annotations while maintaining competitive accuracy compared with fully supervised methods. Among various weak annotations, box annotation offers an ideal trade-off between labeling efficiency and supervision strength. However, most box-supervised methods rely on a two-stage training pipeline: 1) generating pseudo-labels, 2) training the segmentation model with pseudo-labels, which is iterative and sensitive to pseudo-label quality. To address this issue, we propose an end-to-end framework that directly learns instance masks from box annotations without an explicit pseudo-label generation and iterative relabeling and retraining stage. Specifically, we introduce a boundary-aware refinement module that adaptively learns instance boundaries from boxes through level set evolution. Furthermore, we propose a multi-scale geometric augmentation module to alleviate semantic ambiguity in overlapping regions by applying cross-view consistency constraints on predictions. Finally, we construct a multi-objective optimization framework, which improves both the training stability of level-set-evolution-based boundary refinement and the overall segmentation performance. Extensive experimental results on both indoor and outdoor datasets demonstrate that our method achieves the SOTA performance across multiple benchmark datasets with different backbones and closely approaches fully supervised counterparts.

item.title: RCACF: 4D Radar-Camera Adaptive and Cooperative Fusion for 3D detection
item.dc:creator: Shihao Wang
Keyan Ren
Wenzhuo Zhu
Jingyi Huang
Jianyu Gu
Qing Niu
item.description: Fusing 4D radar and camera data shows greater potential for 3D object detection in autonomous driving systems. However, existing approaches are constrained by static fusion rules and isolated modality processing, leading to limited environmental and inadequate cross-modal complementarity. In an attempt to tackle these problems, we introduce a fast yet effective framework for adaptive and cooperative fusion 4D radar-camera fusion called RCACF. The framework comprises two key components designed to address specific fusion bottlenecks. First, to overcome the information loss caused by radar sparsity, we propose the Radar-assisted Feature Extractor (RFE). By utilizing a physics-aware adaptive diffusion mechanism, RFE densifies radar cues in the region of interest, significantly enhancing detection robustness in adverse weather conditions. Second, to resolve cross-modal spatial misalignment in dynamic scenes, we introduce the Heatmap-guided Feature Aggregation (HFA). This module establishes precise cross-modal correspondence through dual-branch deformable attention,boosting dynamic object detection accuracy by compensating for sensor asynchrony. The framework then performs hierarchical fusion using two specialized components: NFN (Norm-FFN-Norm) for channel-wise feature integration and CBR (Conv-BatchNorm-Relu) for spatial feature enhancement. The proposed RCACF framework is validated on both the nuScenes and View-of-Delft (VoD) datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that our method outperforms the baseline method CRN by 1.2% mAP on nuScenes and achieves a 0.39% mAP improvement over the baseline method RCBEVDet on VoD, while having a faster inference speed at 21 ∼ 30 " role="presentation"> 21 ∼ 30 21 ∼ 30 FPS. Published: 2026-04-21T06:52:16+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.788 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Shihao Wang; Keyan Ren; Wenzhuo Zhu; Jingyi Huang; Jianyu Gu; Qing Niu

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.113778

Relevance: 0.788 (must_read)

Fusing 4D radar and camera data shows greater potential for 3D object detection in autonomous driving systems. However, existing approaches are constrained by static fusion rules and isolated modality processing, leading to limited environmental and inadequate cross-modal complementarity. In an attempt to tackle these problems, we introduce a fast yet effective framework for adaptive and cooperative fusion 4D radar-camera fusion called RCACF. The framework comprises two key components designed to address specific fusion bottlenecks. First, to overcome the information loss caused by radar sparsity, we propose the Radar-assisted Feature Extractor (RFE). By utilizing a physics-aware adaptive diffusion mechanism, RFE densifies radar cues in the region of interest, significantly enhancing detection robustness in adverse weather conditions. Second, to resolve cross-modal spatial misalignment in dynamic scenes, we introduce the Heatmap-guided Feature Aggregation (HFA). This module establishes precise cross-modal correspondence through dual-branch deformable attention,boosting dynamic object detection accuracy by compensating for sensor asynchrony. The framework then performs hierarchical fusion using two specialized components: NFN (Norm-FFN-Norm) for channel-wise feature integration and CBR (Conv-BatchNorm-Relu) for spatial feature enhancement. The proposed RCACF framework is validated on both the nuScenes and View-of-Delft (VoD) datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that our method outperforms the baseline method CRN by 1.2% mAP on nuScenes and achieves a 0.39% mAP improvement over the baseline method RCBEVDet on VoD, while having a faster inference speed at 21 ∼ 30 " role="presentation"> 21 ∼ 30 21 ∼ 30 FPS.

item.title: Visible and Infrared Image Fusion Based on Adaptive Weighted Multimodal Features Extraction and Bidirectional Guidance Structure
item.dc:creator: Ziyi Chen
Gaosheng Cai
Dilong Li
Jing Wang
Jin Gou
Kai Chen
Cheng Wang
Jonathan Li
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: Efficient fusion of infrared and visible images is of critical importance for real-time applications such as autonomous driving. While deep learning-based fusion methods have demonstrated significant improvements in fusion quality in recent years, current network architectures still exhibit unsatisfactory computational complexity and processing speed. To reduce computational complexity and improve fusion efficiency, mask-based methods or approaches driven by downstream tasks often prioritize key regions. However, such methods tend to overemphasize target objects, potentially overlooking contextually significant elements. To address this limitation and achieve more effective fusion, we propose BEFuse, a decoupled two-stage training strategy with an end-to-end inference framework. BEFuse extracts shallow features and gradient information from images in the first stage via a cross-modal image segmentation subnetwork. In the fusion stage, we use the Hadamard product to map features to an implicit quadratic feature space, combining feature similarity and gradient mask information, allowing automatic adjustment of loss weights and improving fusion accuracy. Experiments on four datasets (MSRS, TNO, RoadScene, and M3FD) show that BEFuse outperforms existing methods in both fusion quality and computational speed. Published: 2026-04-20T20:06:49+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.786 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Ziyi Chen; Gaosheng Cai; Dilong Li; Jing Wang; Jin Gou; Kai Chen; Cheng Wang; Jonathan Li

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3685790

Relevance: 0.786 (must_read)

Efficient fusion of infrared and visible images is of critical importance for real-time applications such as autonomous driving. While deep learning-based fusion methods have demonstrated significant improvements in fusion quality in recent years, current network architectures still exhibit unsatisfactory computational complexity and processing speed. To reduce computational complexity and improve fusion efficiency, mask-based methods or approaches driven by downstream tasks often prioritize key regions. However, such methods tend to overemphasize target objects, potentially overlooking contextually significant elements. To address this limitation and achieve more effective fusion, we propose BEFuse, a decoupled two-stage training strategy with an end-to-end inference framework. BEFuse extracts shallow features and gradient information from images in the first stage via a cross-modal image segmentation subnetwork. In the fusion stage, we use the Hadamard product to map features to an implicit quadratic feature space, combining feature similarity and gradient mask information, allowing automatic adjustment of loss weights and improving fusion accuracy. Experiments on four datasets (MSRS, TNO, RoadScene, and M3FD) show that BEFuse outperforms existing methods in both fusion quality and computational speed.

item.title: LAFI-Diffusion：基于可控扩散模型的卫星遥感舰船目标检测数据集构建与生成方法
item.dc:creator: HE Da
LI Zeyu
LIU Haoran
HU Xikun
ZHONG Ping
SHI Qian
item.description: 2026年4月21日国防科技大学 自动目标识别全国重点实验室的胡锡坤团队和中山大学 地理科学与规划学院的何达团队在《遥感学报》发文，介绍了其在卫星遥感图像舰船目标检测与识别领域的研究进展，胡锡坤和何达专家构建了大规模细粒度舰船实例检测数据集LAFI及百万级舰船目标检测数据集LAFI - Diffusion，为突破该领域数据瓶颈提供解决方案。 Published: 2026-04-21T08:59:47+00:00 Venue: National Remote Sensing Bulletin Score: 0.784 (must_read)

Journal: National Remote Sensing Bulletin

Relevance: 0.784 (must_read)

2026年4月21日国防科技大学 自动目标识别全国重点实验室的胡锡坤团队和中山大学 地理科学与规划学院的何达团队在《遥感学报》发文，介绍了其在卫星遥感图像舰船目标检测与识别领域的研究进展，胡锡坤和何达专家构建了大规模细粒度舰船实例检测数据集LAFI及百万级舰船目标检测数据集LAFI - Diffusion，为突破该领域数据瓶颈提供解决方案。

item.title: Large vision-language model knowledge guided multi-source urban land-use mapping: A case study of representative cities across six continents
item.dc:creator: Ruiyi Yang
Yu Su
Yinhe Liu
Ailong Ma
Yanfei Zhong
item.prism:publicationName: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
item.description: Urban land-use maps provide critical insights into the functional structure and urbanization process of cities. While high-resolution remote sensing (HRS) images offer detailed information on ground objects, they are insufficient for comprehensive land-use analysis, as land-use patterns involve both natural and socio-economic factors. To address this, integrating HRS images with points of interest (POIs) reflecting human activities has become a common approach. However, variations in physical appearances and POI density across cities pose challenges for large-scale urban land-use mapping. In this study, a large vision-language model knowledge-guided multi-source feature learning framework for urban land-use mapping (VLM-LU) is proposed. By leveraging a large vision-language model (VLM), the framework generates captions robust to environmental changes. It incorporates a voting-based mechanism and rule-based refinement to mitigate VLM hallucinations. Additionally, a joint object-guided visual representation refinement (JGR) module is introduced to improve robustness against variations in urban landscapes by guiding the visual encoder to focus on key areas. A confidence-aware gating (CAG) module further enhances performance by weighting the relative importance of each modality to obtain an informative fused representation. Experimental results on the Multi-CUN dataset and representative cities across six continents demonstrate that the proposed VLM-LU framework significantly improves the generalizability and robustness of large-scale urban land-use mapping. In several study areas, the overall accuracy (OA) of VLM-LU exceeds state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods by more than 10%. Even with limited annotated samples, VLM-LU still performs well, demonstrating its potential practicality in large-scale land-use mapping. The source code is available at https://github.com/Rayoll/VLM-LU . Published: 2026-04-21T19:18:36+00:00 Venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Score: 0.782 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Ruiyi Yang; Yu Su; Yinhe Liu; Ailong Ma; Yanfei Zhong

Journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1016/j.isprsjprs.2026.04.004

Relevance: 0.782 (must_read)

Urban land-use maps provide critical insights into the functional structure and urbanization process of cities. While high-resolution remote sensing (HRS) images offer detailed information on ground objects, they are insufficient for comprehensive land-use analysis, as land-use patterns involve both natural and socio-economic factors. To address this, integrating HRS images with points of interest (POIs) reflecting human activities has become a common approach. However, variations in physical appearances and POI density across cities pose challenges for large-scale urban land-use mapping. In this study, a large vision-language model knowledge-guided multi-source feature learning framework for urban land-use mapping (VLM-LU) is proposed. By leveraging a large vision-language model (VLM), the framework generates captions robust to environmental changes. It incorporates a voting-based mechanism and rule-based refinement to mitigate VLM hallucinations. Additionally, a joint object-guided visual representation refinement (JGR) module is introduced to improve robustness against variations in urban landscapes by guiding the visual encoder to focus on key areas. A confidence-aware gating (CAG) module further enhances performance by weighting the relative importance of each modality to obtain an informative fused representation. Experimental results on the Multi-CUN dataset and representative cities across six continents demonstrate that the proposed VLM-LU framework significantly improves the generalizability and robustness of large-scale urban land-use mapping. In several study areas, the overall accuracy (OA) of VLM-LU exceeds state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods by more than 10%. Even with limited annotated samples, VLM-LU still performs well, demonstrating its potential practicality in large-scale land-use mapping. The source code is available at https://github.com/Rayoll/VLM-LU .

item.title: PDDA: Prompt-driven Domain Adaptation for Real-world Image Dehazing
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhang
Yuxin Feng
Xin Li
Fan Zhou
Zhuo Su
item.description: Due to the complexity and diversity of practical environments, real-world image dehazing remains an unresolved problem, with one of the key challenges being how to bridge the distribution gap between synthetic and real domains. This paper proposes a Prompt-driven Domain Adaptation (PDDA) framework within the bi-level optimization perspective. Specifically, we introduce hyperparameter optimization-based bi-level modeling: the lower-level optimization emphasizes prior learning within the synthetic domain to stabilize dehazing performance, while the upper-level optimization focuses on enhancing cross-domain adaptability to ensure that the model can generalize across different domains. Given the scarcity of paired real haze images, we train learnable haze prompts by jointly optimizing the text-image similarity between positive/negative prompts and corresponding clear/haze images in the CLIP latent space to more effectively capture real-world haze characteristics. Based on the learned haze prompts, we construct an unsupervised cross-domain loss function that enhances the adaptability to complex real-world scenarios by integrating prompt learning with bi-level optimization strategy. Furthermore, we conduct a comprehensive exploration to uncover the inherent properties of PDDA, including architecture-irrelevant flexibility and domain-agnostic robustness. Extensive experiments across a wide range of benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method achieves both quantitative and qualitative improvements across diverse scenarios, showing robust performance not only in real-world daytime conditions but also exhibiting superior cross-domain adaptation capabilities in nighttime scenarios. Codes are available at https://github.com/YanZhang-zy/PDDA.git. Published: 2026-04-21T19:51:35+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.782 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yan Zhang; Yuxin Feng; Xin Li; Fan Zhou; Zhuo Su

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3684402

Relevance: 0.782 (must_read)

Due to the complexity and diversity of practical environments, real-world image dehazing remains an unresolved problem, with one of the key challenges being how to bridge the distribution gap between synthetic and real domains. This paper proposes a Prompt-driven Domain Adaptation (PDDA) framework within the bi-level optimization perspective. Specifically, we introduce hyperparameter optimization-based bi-level modeling: the lower-level optimization emphasizes prior learning within the synthetic domain to stabilize dehazing performance, while the upper-level optimization focuses on enhancing cross-domain adaptability to ensure that the model can generalize across different domains. Given the scarcity of paired real haze images, we train learnable haze prompts by jointly optimizing the text-image similarity between positive/negative prompts and corresponding clear/haze images in the CLIP latent space to more effectively capture real-world haze characteristics. Based on the learned haze prompts, we construct an unsupervised cross-domain loss function that enhances the adaptability to complex real-world scenarios by integrating prompt learning with bi-level optimization strategy. Furthermore, we conduct a comprehensive exploration to uncover the inherent properties of PDDA, including architecture-irrelevant flexibility and domain-agnostic robustness. Extensive experiments across a wide range of benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method achieves both quantitative and qualitative improvements across diverse scenarios, showing robust performance not only in real-world daytime conditions but also exhibiting superior cross-domain adaptation capabilities in nighttime scenarios. Codes are available at https://github.com/YanZhang-zy/PDDA.git.

item.title: Prompting Foundation Models for Zero-Shot Ship Instance Segmentation in SAR Imagery
item.dc:creator: Islam Mansour
Francescopaolo Sica
Michael Schmitt
item.description: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) plays a critical role in maritime surveillance, yet deep learning for SAR analysis is limited by the lack of pixel-level annotations. This paper explores how general-purpose vision foundation models can enable zero-shot ship instance segmentation in SAR imagery, eliminating the need for pixel-level supervision. A YOLOv11-based detector trained on open SAR datasets localizes ships via bounding boxes, which then prompt the Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM2) to produce instance masks without any mask annotations. Unlike prior SAM-based SAR approaches that rely on fine tuning or adapters, our method demonstrates that spatial constraints from a SAR-trained detector alone can effectively regularize foundation model predictions. This design partially mitigates the optical-SAR domain gap and enables downstream applications such as vessel classification, size estimation, and wake analysis. Experiments on the SSDD benchmark achieve a mean IoU of 0.637 (89% of a fully supervised baseline) with an overall ship detection rate of 89.2%, confirming a scalable, annotation-efficient pathway toward foundation-model-driven SAR image understanding. Published: 2026-04-20T07:57:11+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.782 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Islam Mansour; Francescopaolo Sica; Michael Schmitt

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.782 (must_read)

Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) plays a critical role in maritime surveillance, yet deep learning for SAR analysis is limited by the lack of pixel-level annotations. This paper explores how general-purpose vision foundation models can enable zero-shot ship instance segmentation in SAR imagery, eliminating the need for pixel-level supervision. A YOLOv11-based detector trained on open SAR datasets localizes ships via bounding boxes, which then prompt the Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM2) to produce instance masks without any mask annotations. Unlike prior SAM-based SAR approaches that rely on fine tuning or adapters, our method demonstrates that spatial constraints from a SAR-trained detector alone can effectively regularize foundation model predictions. This design partially mitigates the optical-SAR domain gap and enables downstream applications such as vessel classification, size estimation, and wake analysis. Experiments on the SSDD benchmark achieve a mean IoU of 0.637 (89% of a fully supervised baseline) with an overall ship detection rate of 89.2%, confirming a scalable, annotation-efficient pathway toward foundation-model-driven SAR image understanding.

item.title: Better with Less: Tackling Heterogeneous Multi-Modal Image Joint Pretraining via Conditioned and Degraded Masked Autoencoder
item.dc:creator: Bowen Peng
Yongxiang Liu
Jie Zhou
Xiaodong Chen
Tianpeng Liu
Xiaogang Yu
Li Liu
item.description: Learning robust representations across extremely heterogeneous modalities remains a fundamental challenge in multi-modal vision. As a critical and profound instantiation of this challenge, high-resolution (HR) joint optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) pretraining seeks modality synergy to mutually enhance single-source representations; its potential is severely hindered by the Heterogeneity-Resolution Paradox: finer spatial scales drastically amplify the physical divergence between complex radar geometries and non-homologous optical textures. Consequently, migrating medium-resolution-oriented rigid alignment paradigms to HR scenarios triggers either severe feature suppression to force equivalence, or feature contamination driven by extreme epistemic uncertainty. Both extremes inevitably culminate in profound representation degradation and negative transfer. To overcome this bottleneck, we propose CoDe-MAE, pioneering a \textit{better synergy with less alignment} philosophy. First, Optical-anchored Knowledge Distillation (OKD) implicitly regularizes SAR's speckle noise by mapping it into a pure semantic manifold. Building on this, Conditioned Contrastive Learning (CCL) utilizes a gradient buffering mechanism to align shared consensus while safely preserving divergent physical signatures. Concurrently, Cross-Modal Degraded Reconstruction (CDR) deliberately strips non-homologous spectral pseudo-features, truncating the inherently ill-posed mapping to capture true structural invariants. Extensive analyses validate our theoretical claims. Pretrained on 1M samples, CoDe-MAE demonstrates remarkable data efficiency, successfully preventing representation degradation and establishing new state-of-the-art performance across diverse single- and bi-modal downstream tasks, substantially outperforming foundation models scaled on vastly larger datasets. Published: 2026-04-18T10:23:00+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.781 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Bowen Peng; Yongxiang Liu; Jie Zhou; Xiaodong Chen; Tianpeng Liu; Xiaogang Yu; Li Liu

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.781 (must_read)

Learning robust representations across extremely heterogeneous modalities remains a fundamental challenge in multi-modal vision. As a critical and profound instantiation of this challenge, high-resolution (HR) joint optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) pretraining seeks modality synergy to mutually enhance single-source representations; its potential is severely hindered by the Heterogeneity-Resolution Paradox: finer spatial scales drastically amplify the physical divergence between complex radar geometries and non-homologous optical textures. Consequently, migrating medium-resolution-oriented rigid alignment paradigms to HR scenarios triggers either severe feature suppression to force equivalence, or feature contamination driven by extreme epistemic uncertainty. Both extremes inevitably culminate in profound representation degradation and negative transfer. To overcome this bottleneck, we propose CoDe-MAE, pioneering a \textit{better synergy with less alignment} philosophy. First, Optical-anchored Knowledge Distillation (OKD) implicitly regularizes SAR's speckle noise by mapping it into a pure semantic manifold. Building on this, Conditioned Contrastive Learning (CCL) utilizes a gradient buffering mechanism to align shared consensus while safely preserving divergent physical signatures. Concurrently, Cross-Modal Degraded Reconstruction (CDR) deliberately strips non-homologous spectral pseudo-features, truncating the inherently ill-posed mapping to capture true structural invariants. Extensive analyses validate our theoretical claims. Pretrained on 1M samples, CoDe-MAE demonstrates remarkable data efficiency, successfully preventing representation degradation and establishing new state-of-the-art performance across diverse single- and bi-modal downstream tasks, substantially outperforming foundation models scaled on vastly larger datasets.

item.title: Learning Monocular Depth via Cascaded Iterative Refinement in Visual-Echo Scenes
item.dc:creator: Anjie Wang
Zhijun Fang
Leidong Fan
Ying Zhou
Guibiao Liao
Siwei Ma
Jenq-Neng Hwang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: In recent years, integrating multimodal information, particularly visual and echo data, has shown great promise for improving depth estimation performance. While existing works demonstrate that combining binaural echo features with image attributes can enhance depth estimation, they often use rudimentary feature alignment and fusion methods, failing to fully exploit the complementary nature of cross-modal information and limiting integration effectiveness. To address these challenges, this paper introduces an innovative multimodal fusion framework. First, the framework incorporates a combination of multi-scale self-attention and cross-attention mechanisms, establishing correlations between features and facilitating cohesive interactions between the visual and echo domains. Furthermore, we propose an incremental feature updating mechanism based on Convolutional Gated Recurrent Units (ConvGRU), which implements cascaded iterative optimization, integrating contextual features with the multi-scale fused features from both echo and image modalities. In each iteration, the framework preserves contextual information from previous steps while employing a multi-level loss function to guide result updates. This approach effectively captures spatial structural information and progressively enhances depth estimation accuracy. Comprehensive experimental evaluations on the Replica, Matterport3D and BatVision (BV1) datasets validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. Comparative analyses with state-of-the-art monocular plus echo methods underscore the superior performance achievable through this novel framework. Published: 2026-04-20T20:06:49+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.778 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Anjie Wang; Zhijun Fang; Leidong Fan; Ying Zhou; Guibiao Liao; Siwei Ma; Jenq-Neng Hwang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3685328

Relevance: 0.778 (must_read)

In recent years, integrating multimodal information, particularly visual and echo data, has shown great promise for improving depth estimation performance. While existing works demonstrate that combining binaural echo features with image attributes can enhance depth estimation, they often use rudimentary feature alignment and fusion methods, failing to fully exploit the complementary nature of cross-modal information and limiting integration effectiveness. To address these challenges, this paper introduces an innovative multimodal fusion framework. First, the framework incorporates a combination of multi-scale self-attention and cross-attention mechanisms, establishing correlations between features and facilitating cohesive interactions between the visual and echo domains. Furthermore, we propose an incremental feature updating mechanism based on Convolutional Gated Recurrent Units (ConvGRU), which implements cascaded iterative optimization, integrating contextual features with the multi-scale fused features from both echo and image modalities. In each iteration, the framework preserves contextual information from previous steps while employing a multi-level loss function to guide result updates. This approach effectively captures spatial structural information and progressively enhances depth estimation accuracy. Comprehensive experimental evaluations on the Replica, Matterport3D and BatVision (BV1) datasets validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. Comparative analyses with state-of-the-art monocular plus echo methods underscore the superior performance achievable through this novel framework.

item.title: Navigating Score Matching within Diffusion Models
item.dc:creator: Junyu Zhang
Daochang Liu
Shichao Zhang
Chang Xu
item.description: Diffusion models (DMs) have achieved remarkable generative performance, revolutionizing advancements in computer vision. Unfortunately, image quality is affected by inaccurate scores when the sampling trajectory gets stuck in low-density regions between disjoint modes, as these areas suffer from a scarcity of training data. To alleviate this, we put forward a hybrid generative-discriminative training framework designed to develop score direction navigation abilities. Specifically, our framework simultaneously optimizes both tasks using the given noisy input images, enabling the score to learn its direction by distinguishing its corresponding cluster mode. In this context, during sampling, the improved score guides the sampling trajectory through the high-density regions of its corresponding mode, thus avoiding inaccurate scores. Following this design philosophy, image quality can be naturally improved through the learned directional navigation abilities, without affecting sampling diversity. Extensive experimental results on four widely used datasets and various diffusion model architectures demonstrate the effectiveness and cost-efficiency of our framework. Published: 2026-04-21T19:50:08+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia Score: 0.777 (must_read)

Authors: Junyu Zhang; Daochang Liu; Shichao Zhang; Chang Xu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia

DOI: 10.1109/tmm.2026.3685791

Relevance: 0.777 (must_read)

Diffusion models (DMs) have achieved remarkable generative performance, revolutionizing advancements in computer vision. Unfortunately, image quality is affected by inaccurate scores when the sampling trajectory gets stuck in low-density regions between disjoint modes, as these areas suffer from a scarcity of training data. To alleviate this, we put forward a hybrid generative-discriminative training framework designed to develop score direction navigation abilities. Specifically, our framework simultaneously optimizes both tasks using the given noisy input images, enabling the score to learn its direction by distinguishing its corresponding cluster mode. In this context, during sampling, the improved score guides the sampling trajectory through the high-density regions of its corresponding mode, thus avoiding inaccurate scores. Following this design philosophy, image quality can be naturally improved through the learned directional navigation abilities, without affecting sampling diversity. Extensive experimental results on four widely used datasets and various diffusion model architectures demonstrate the effectiveness and cost-efficiency of our framework.

item.title: FSSG: Generative few-shot object detection via style-geometry fusion
item.dc:creator: Jing Liu
Yujin Zheng
Xu Guo
Chu He
Dingwen Wang
item.description: Few-shot object detectors are typically trained on an abundance of base-class samples, making it difficult to integrate knowledge from a limited number of novel samples into a highly generalized model. This often leads to a bias toward the base classes, and the resulting model can be overly sensitive to variations in novel samples. To address these challenges, we propose FSSG, a unified few-shot object detection framework that explicitly separates category-relevant semantic structure from stylistic appearance variation. Within this unified framework, appearance diversity and structural consistency are jointly optimized across three interdependent stages: 1) A feature augmentation mechanism that introduces controllable appearance diversity while preserving category-relevant semantic structure; 2) A dual-branch architecture where geometric structural correction operates in tandem with the augmentation branch to rectify spatial misalignments and align sparse foreground correspondences; and 3) An Excitation–Suppression Module that regularizes the highly perturbed feature space by enforcing angular-margin constraints, ensuring compact intra-class clustering and clear inter-class separation. Our approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods across all shot levels and data splits, achieving improvements of +3.8% on the PASCAL VOC benchmark and +3.3% on the challenging COCO benchmark. Published: 2026-04-20T16:11:18+00:00 Venue: Neurocomputing Score: 0.776 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jing Liu; Yujin Zheng; Xu Guo; Chu He; Dingwen Wang

Journal: Neurocomputing

DOI: 10.1016/j.neucom.2026.133749

Relevance: 0.776 (must_read)

Few-shot object detectors are typically trained on an abundance of base-class samples, making it difficult to integrate knowledge from a limited number of novel samples into a highly generalized model. This often leads to a bias toward the base classes, and the resulting model can be overly sensitive to variations in novel samples. To address these challenges, we propose FSSG, a unified few-shot object detection framework that explicitly separates category-relevant semantic structure from stylistic appearance variation. Within this unified framework, appearance diversity and structural consistency are jointly optimized across three interdependent stages: 1) A feature augmentation mechanism that introduces controllable appearance diversity while preserving category-relevant semantic structure; 2) A dual-branch architecture where geometric structural correction operates in tandem with the augmentation branch to rectify spatial misalignments and align sparse foreground correspondences; and 3) An Excitation–Suppression Module that regularizes the highly perturbed feature space by enforcing angular-margin constraints, ensuring compact intra-class clustering and clear inter-class separation. Our approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods across all shot levels and data splits, achieving improvements of +3.8% on the PASCAL VOC benchmark and +3.3% on the challenging COCO benchmark.

item.title: AlignMLLA: A Lightweight Dual-stage Alignment Fusion Network for Infrared-visible Image Fusion
item.dc:creator: Lijun Yuan
Ming Yang
Qiqin Li
Chuanjiang Xie
Xinyu Zhu
item.description: Visible-infrared (RGB-IR) fusion for object detection is prone to instability in small-object and low-contrast scenarios due to local geometric misalignment and global distribution shift. To mitigate these effects, a detection baseline is constructed to retain modality-specific information via dual-parallel RGB-IR feature extraction, with a direction-attention partial C2f (DA-PC2f) embedded in the IR branch to strengthen shallow thermal-detail representation at negligible cost. On this baseline, a lightweight dual-stage alignment fusion network (AlignMLLA) is proposed: local token-level alignment is first performed via Optimal Transport to correct geometric mismatch, and global distribution alignment loss is then enforced during training by applying a Maximum Mean Discrepancy to mitigate modality shift. The aligned unimodal features are subsequently fused by a Mamba-Like Linear Attention design with confidence-aware gating. Extensive experiments on three representative datasets demonstrate that, relative to state-of-the-art multimodal approaches, our method provides a more favorable efficiency-accuracy trade-off: competitive detection performance is achieved while the parameter count and computational cost are reduced by up to 94% and 92%, respectively. Moreover, compared with the strongest lightweight unimodal baseline YOLOv12n, the mean average precision (mAP) is increased by 1%–3%. The source code is available at https://github.com/yuan95766-spec/AlignMLLA . Published: 2026-04-21T23:27:49+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.775 (must_read)

Authors: Lijun Yuan; Ming Yang; Qiqin Li; Chuanjiang Xie; Xinyu Zhu

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.113800

Relevance: 0.775 (must_read)

Visible-infrared (RGB-IR) fusion for object detection is prone to instability in small-object and low-contrast scenarios due to local geometric misalignment and global distribution shift. To mitigate these effects, a detection baseline is constructed to retain modality-specific information via dual-parallel RGB-IR feature extraction, with a direction-attention partial C2f (DA-PC2f) embedded in the IR branch to strengthen shallow thermal-detail representation at negligible cost. On this baseline, a lightweight dual-stage alignment fusion network (AlignMLLA) is proposed: local token-level alignment is first performed via Optimal Transport to correct geometric mismatch, and global distribution alignment loss is then enforced during training by applying a Maximum Mean Discrepancy to mitigate modality shift. The aligned unimodal features are subsequently fused by a Mamba-Like Linear Attention design with confidence-aware gating. Extensive experiments on three representative datasets demonstrate that, relative to state-of-the-art multimodal approaches, our method provides a more favorable efficiency-accuracy trade-off: competitive detection performance is achieved while the parameter count and computational cost are reduced by up to 94% and 92%, respectively. Moreover, compared with the strongest lightweight unimodal baseline YOLOv12n, the mean average precision (mAP) is increased by 1%–3%. The source code is available at https://github.com/yuan95766-spec/AlignMLLA .

item.title: RSTFA: Efficient Training-Free Human Preference Alignment via Rejection Sampling for Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
item.dc:creator: Hongzheng Yang
Jason Chun-Lok Li
Li Kun
Wenao Ma
Mingjie Xu
Yuzhi Zhao
Lai-Man Po
item.description: Given a text-to-image diffusion model pretrained on large-scale text-image pairs, can we align the model with human preferences without further fine-tuning? In this paper, we analyze the effect of alignment tuning in diffusion models by comparing the diffusion denoising trajectory between base and aligned models. Our findings reveal that alignment tuning primarily affects superficial stylistic aspects during denoising, rather than fundamental content, suggesting superficial alignment behaviors. Based on this discovery, we introduce a novel, training-free alignment approach (RSTFA) that leverages rejection sampling at specific stylistic timesteps, ensuring human preference alignment without fine-tuning or heavy inference overhead. We provide a theoretical analysis and derive a bias bound for our rejection-sampling alignment scheme. Empirically, we show that RSTFA better preserves sample diversity than reinforcement-learning-based tuning methods. Extensive experiments on Pick-a-Pic, COCO, HPD V2, and PartiPrompts show that our method not only achieves superior alignment with human preferences compared to state-of-the-art methods, but also reduces computational demands, establishing efficient, human-centered diffusion model alignment. Published: 2026-04-21T19:51:35+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.774 (must_read)

Authors: Hongzheng Yang; Jason Chun-Lok Li; Li Kun; Wenao Ma; Mingjie Xu; Yuzhi Zhao; Lai-Man Po

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3684401

Relevance: 0.774 (must_read)

Given a text-to-image diffusion model pretrained on large-scale text-image pairs, can we align the model with human preferences without further fine-tuning? In this paper, we analyze the effect of alignment tuning in diffusion models by comparing the diffusion denoising trajectory between base and aligned models. Our findings reveal that alignment tuning primarily affects superficial stylistic aspects during denoising, rather than fundamental content, suggesting superficial alignment behaviors. Based on this discovery, we introduce a novel, training-free alignment approach (RSTFA) that leverages rejection sampling at specific stylistic timesteps, ensuring human preference alignment without fine-tuning or heavy inference overhead. We provide a theoretical analysis and derive a bias bound for our rejection-sampling alignment scheme. Empirically, we show that RSTFA better preserves sample diversity than reinforcement-learning-based tuning methods. Extensive experiments on Pick-a-Pic, COCO, HPD V2, and PartiPrompts show that our method not only achieves superior alignment with human preferences compared to state-of-the-art methods, but also reduces computational demands, establishing efficient, human-centered diffusion model alignment.

item.title: FSMD–Net: Joint Spatial–Channel Spectral Modeling for SAR Ship Detection in Complex Inshore Scenarios
item.dc:creator: Xianxun Yao
Yijiang Shen
Yuheng Lei
item.description: Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) ship detection in complex inshore scenarios has long been constrained by the coupled effects of speckle noise and small–scale weak scattering targets. Although feature–level frequency–domain denoising methods partially alleviate noise interference, existing studies predominantly focus on spatial frequency modeling and implicitly assume consistent spectral responses and discriminative contributions across channels. This assumption may lead to over–suppression of weak ship targets under complex backgrounds. To address the incomplete dimensionality of current frequency–domain modeling, this paper proposes FSMD–Net, a joint spatial–channel spectral modeling framework for SAR ship detection. During multi–scale feature fusion, a coordinated modulation mechanism integrating multi–spectral channel attention with spatial frequency–domain denoising is introduced. This design enables channel discriminability and frequency–subspace denoising to act synergistically, enforcing structurally consistent spectral constraints throughout multi–scale feature propagation. Extensive experiments on SARDet–100K, HRSID, and AIR–SARShip–2.0 demonstrate that FSMD–Net achieves consistent performance improvements, particularly in small–target and strong–clutter scenarios, exhibiting enhanced detection accuracy and robustness. Published: 2026-04-21T16:10:44+00:00 Venue: Remote Sensing Score: 0.774 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xianxun Yao; Yijiang Shen; Yuheng Lei

Journal: Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.3390/rs18081254

Relevance: 0.774 (must_read)

Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) ship detection in complex inshore scenarios has long been constrained by the coupled effects of speckle noise and small–scale weak scattering targets. Although feature–level frequency–domain denoising methods partially alleviate noise interference, existing studies predominantly focus on spatial frequency modeling and implicitly assume consistent spectral responses and discriminative contributions across channels. This assumption may lead to over–suppression of weak ship targets under complex backgrounds. To address the incomplete dimensionality of current frequency–domain modeling, this paper proposes FSMD–Net, a joint spatial–channel spectral modeling framework for SAR ship detection. During multi–scale feature fusion, a coordinated modulation mechanism integrating multi–spectral channel attention with spatial frequency–domain denoising is introduced. This design enables channel discriminability and frequency–subspace denoising to act synergistically, enforcing structurally consistent spectral constraints throughout multi–scale feature propagation. Extensive experiments on SARDet–100K, HRSID, and AIR–SARShip–2.0 demonstrate that FSMD–Net achieves consistent performance improvements, particularly in small–target and strong–clutter scenarios, exhibiting enhanced detection accuracy and robustness.

item.title: Energy Diffusion Prior for Infrared Small Target Detection: Build Representations for Objects instead of Scenarios
item.dc:creator: Yunfei Tong
Hai Yang
Ganghao Liu
Zhe Wang
item.description: Infrared small target detection aims to recognize and locate distant targets from thermal images obtained by infrared imaging systems. The small proportion of distant targets in the image makes it a challenging task to achieve accurate and effective target representation. In this paper, we propose an efficient detection model called DPNet based on the principle of infrared imaging. This model provides a more effective representation for small targets through feature recovery and curvature separation. Specifically, the feature recovery network retains the attention of small targets in deep networks by utilizing a simple encoding space and a multi-stage decoding space, reducing redundant skip connections. The curvature separation module leverages the thermal radiation diffusion properties of small targets as prior knowledge and extracts key target features through a learnable discrete Fourier transform. By reducing redundancy and incorporating prior knowledge, our network can learn effective target features for the detection task more efficiently and purposefully, rather than relying on complex networks to learn the variable backgrounds in different scenes. Experiments on multiple public datasets demonstrate that our proposed method is more efficient and accurate than state-of-the-art methods. Code is available at https://github.com/tarayatong/EnergyDiffusionPrior . Published: 2026-04-21T06:59:35+00:00 Venue: Expert Systems with Applications Score: 0.773 (must_read)

Infrared small target detection aims to recognize and locate distant targets from thermal images obtained by infrared imaging systems. The small proportion of distant targets in the image makes it a challenging task to achieve accurate and effective target representation. In this paper, we propose an efficient detection model called DPNet based on the principle of infrared imaging. This model provides a more effective representation for small targets through feature recovery and curvature separation. Specifically, the feature recovery network retains the attention of small targets in deep networks by utilizing a simple encoding space and a multi-stage decoding space, reducing redundant skip connections. The curvature separation module leverages the thermal radiation diffusion properties of small targets as prior knowledge and extracts key target features through a learnable discrete Fourier transform. By reducing redundancy and incorporating prior knowledge, our network can learn effective target features for the detection task more efficiently and purposefully, rather than relying on complex networks to learn the variable backgrounds in different scenes. Experiments on multiple public datasets demonstrate that our proposed method is more efficient and accurate than state-of-the-art methods. Code is available at https://github.com/tarayatong/EnergyDiffusionPrior .

item.title: Adv-STMamba: A Lightweight Spatial-Temporal Mamba Model Incorporating Adversarial Training for Radar HRRPTarget Recognition
item.dc:creator: Ke Wang
Xiaoyi Pan
Xiaofeng Ai
Zhiming Xu
Feng Zhao
Shunping Xiao
Siwei Chen
Yafei Song
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
item.description: The electromagnetic space of modern battlefields is becoming increasingly complex. High-Resolution Range Profile (HRRP) has emerged as a core data carrier in the field of Radar Automatic Target Recognition (RATR), due to its advantages of easy acquisition, low storage resource requirements, and high processing efficiency. However, the complex electromagnetic environment inten sifies HRRP's sensitivity to noise and target attitude changes. Existing HRRP recognition methods generally suffer from weak feature extraction capabilities, poor dynamic adaptability, and insufficient robustness in complex environments. To address the aforementioned issues, this paper proposes an Adv-STMamba model, which is built upon a Spatial-Temporal Mamba module (STMamba) and integrates with to dedicated modules: Adversarial Label Smoothing and Adaptive Attention Gate modules. Specifically, Adversarial Label Smoothing resolves the problem of model's overconfidence in labels by generating adversarial soft labels that maximize model loss; STMamba enables in-depth mining of spatiotemporal features in HRRP sequences; Adaptive Attention Gate module achieves efficient spatiotemporal feature fusion by adaptively assigning feature weights, thereby enhancing model's robustness against attitude disturbances. To ensure the persuasiveness of experimental data, this paper converts the public MSTAR dataset into an HRRP dataset and conducts verification through comparative experiments, robustness experiments, complexity experiments, and ablation experiments. The experimental results show that Adv-STMamba model can effectively extract deep-seated features from HRRP data under different signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) scenarios. It achieves a recognition accuracy of 99.67% under the high SNR scenario of 30dB and maintains a recognition accuracy of 97.30% even under the low SNR scenario of 0dB. This fully verifies the effectiveness of Adv-STMamba model in HRRP recognition tasks under complex environments. Published: 2026-04-21T19:51:24+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems Score: 0.773 (must_read)

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems

DOI: 10.1109/taes.2026.3685957

Relevance: 0.773 (must_read)

The electromagnetic space of modern battlefields is becoming increasingly complex. High-Resolution Range Profile (HRRP) has emerged as a core data carrier in the field of Radar Automatic Target Recognition (RATR), due to its advantages of easy acquisition, low storage resource requirements, and high processing efficiency. However, the complex electromagnetic environment inten sifies HRRP's sensitivity to noise and target attitude changes. Existing HRRP recognition methods generally suffer from weak feature extraction capabilities, poor dynamic adaptability, and insufficient robustness in complex environments. To address the aforementioned issues, this paper proposes an Adv-STMamba model, which is built upon a Spatial-Temporal Mamba module (STMamba) and integrates with to dedicated modules: Adversarial Label Smoothing and Adaptive Attention Gate modules. Specifically, Adversarial Label Smoothing resolves the problem of model's overconfidence in labels by generating adversarial soft labels that maximize model loss; STMamba enables in-depth mining of spatiotemporal features in HRRP sequences; Adaptive Attention Gate module achieves efficient spatiotemporal feature fusion by adaptively assigning feature weights, thereby enhancing model's robustness against attitude disturbances. To ensure the persuasiveness of experimental data, this paper converts the public MSTAR dataset into an HRRP dataset and conducts verification through comparative experiments, robustness experiments, complexity experiments, and ablation experiments. The experimental results show that Adv-STMamba model can effectively extract deep-seated features from HRRP data under different signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) scenarios. It achieves a recognition accuracy of 99.67% under the high SNR scenario of 30dB and maintains a recognition accuracy of 97.30% even under the low SNR scenario of 0dB. This fully verifies the effectiveness of Adv-STMamba model in HRRP recognition tasks under complex environments.

item.title: Motion–disturbance decoupling with interaction-aware association for satellite video multi-object tracking
item.dc:creator: Yuman Yuan
Tianyu Deng
Hongyang Bai
Shuai Guo
Yuanbo Chen
item.description: Multi-object tracking (MOT) in satellite video remains challenging because weak target responses, large static backgrounds, and frequent target interactions degrade both detection reliability and data association quality. To address these challenges, a motion-disturbance decoupling network, termed MI-ED, is proposed. The framework models target dynamics with two components: a predictable motion component and an interaction- or occlusion-induced disturbance component. This decoupling is then used to improve detection and association within a unified framework. On the detection side, a motion prior perception module integrates long-term, short-term, and reversed temporal cues, while a motion-guided cross-scale attention feature pyramid network enhances sparse dynamic responses and suppresses static background interference. On the tracking side, an environmental disturbance inference module estimates local interaction risk and recalibrates detection confidence, and a disturbance-aware multi-stage association strategy is used to preserve reliable weak detections and recover targets after short-term misses. Extensive experiments on AIR-MOT and VISO show that, relative to a strong competing method, MI-ED yields MOTA gains of 2.7 and 7.9 points while reducing FP/FN by 19.0%/6.2% and 13.6%/24.4%, respectively, demonstrating strong robustness in dense and occluded satellite scenarios. Published: 2026-04-21T16:06:38+00:00 Venue: Knowledge-Based Systems Score: 0.771 (must_read)
